label: Reporting Person (Whistleblower)
description: A natural person who reports or publicly discloses information on breaches acquired in a work-related context. Includes current and former workers (employees, civil servants, self-employed), shareholders, members of administrative/management/supervisory bodies, volunteers, trainees, contractors, subcontractors, and suppliers. Job applicants who acquired information during recruitment are also included.
responsibilities: Report through internal or external channels in good faith
Ensure information reported concerns a breach within the material scope of the Directive
May choose to report internally, externally, or publicly disclose depending on circumstances

label: Facilitator
description: A natural person who assists a reporting person in the reporting process in a work-related context, whose assistance should be confidential. Facilitators are also protected from retaliation.
responsibilities: Assist the reporting person confidentially if requested
Not be subject to retaliation for providing such assistance

label: Concerned Person
description: A natural or legal person referred to in the report or public disclosure as the person who has allegedly committed the breach or who is allegedly linked to the breach. Must not be treated as automatically guilty and has rights under applicable Union law including the right to an effective remedy and a fair trial.
responsibilities: Rights to defence and confidentiality protection under applicable law

label: Obligated Entity (Legal Entity in Public or Private Sector)
description: A legal entity in the public or private sector required to establish internal reporting channels and follow-up procedures. Private entities with 50+ workers and all public entities are obligated. Must designate a competent person or department to receive reports and follow up.
responsibilities: Establish, operate and maintain internal reporting channels
Designate an impartial person or department to receive and follow up on reports
Acknowledge receipt of reports within 7 days
Follow up and provide feedback to reporting person within 3 months
Maintain confidentiality of reporting person's identity
Maintain records of reports received
Prohibit retaliation against reporting persons and facilitators
Provide clear and accessible information about reporting channels and procedures
Process personal data in accordance with GDPR
source_article: Article 8, Article 9, Article 12, Article 16, Article 17, Article 18

label: Competent Authority
description: Any national authority designated by a Member State to receive reports through external reporting channels and to follow up on those reports. Must have adequate resources and procedures for receiving, following up on, and providing feedback on reports.
responsibilities: Establish external reporting channels
Acknowledge receipt of reports within 7 days
Follow up diligently on reports
Provide feedback to reporting persons within 3 months (extendable to 6 months)
Maintain confidentiality of reporting person's identity
Refer reports outside their remit to appropriate authorities
Publish accessible information about reporting procedures
Cooperate with other competent authorities and EU institutions

label: Member State
description: An EU Member State responsible for transposing the Directive into national law and ensuring its effective implementation, including designating competent authorities and setting effective, proportionate and dissuasive penalties.
responsibilities: Transpose directive obligations into national law by 17 December 2021
Designate competent authorities with external reporting channels
Establish penalties for breaches of the Directive
Provide support measures for reporting persons (legal, financial, psychological)
Take measures to protect reporting persons and third parties from retaliation
Ensure courts can order interim measures to prevent retaliation
Maintain a public register of competent authorities

label: Third Party Affected by Report
description: Persons connected with the reporting person (relatives, colleagues) who could suffer retaliation as a result of the report. Also includes legal entities owned or controlled by the reporting person. These persons are also protected from retaliation.
